nodes.label: Security: Anti-recursion guard (mcp__parallel__run blocked in children)
nodes.norm_label: security: anti-recursion guard (mcp__parallel__run blocked in children)

nodes.norm_label: security: guest sandbox constraints in parallel subtasks

nodes.label: Ask User MCP Server
nodes.description: MCP server exposing ask_user tool. Writes request JSON to /tmp/ask-user-{userId}-{uuid}.json drop-box. Bot polls and renders Telegram inline keyboard buttons. Claude must stop after calling.

nodes.label: ask_user tool
nodes.description: MCP tool: presents options to user as tappable Telegram inline buttons. Requires question (string) and options (array, 2-10 items). Writes drop-box file to /tmp/ask-user-{userId}-{uuid}.json.

nodes.label: Ask User Drop-box Pattern
nodes.description: File-drop pattern: /tmp/ask-user-{userId}-{uuid}.json isolates per-user requests. Bot globs only its own user's files, preventing cross-user leakage.

nodes.label: migrate-guest-public-index.ts
nodes.description: One-shot migration: overwrites /opt/vault/{userId}/public/index.html with current renderHowToSetupGuide() template for existing guests stuck on old placeholder. Detects unmodified placeholders by markers and size (<800 chars). Backs up old file as .bak.{date}. Supports --dry-run and --force flags.

nodes.label: migrate-guest-claude-md.ts
nodes.description: One-shot migration: overwrites CLAUDE.md in every guest vault (/opt/vault/{userId}/CLAUDE.md) with the current generateGuestClaudeMd() template. Takes dated backup before overwriting. Supports --dry-run flag. Skips guests with no existing CLAUDE.md.

nodes.label: migrate-scheduler.ts
nodes.description: One-shot migration: adds bot-scheduler daemon entry to .daemons.yaml for all existing guests. Creates file from scratch if absent, appends entry if present but missing bot-scheduler. Daemon runs /usr/local/bin/bot-scheduler, notifies via NOTIFY_BRIDGE_URL http://172.18.0.1:3849/notify.

nodes.label: migrate-skills.ts
nodes.description: One-shot migration: copies default .md skills from repo skills/ directory into /opt/vault/{userId}/skills/ for every existing guest. Creates skills dir if absent.

nodes.label: import-handoff.ts
nodes.description: One-shot script: reads HANDOFF.md (or arbitrary .md via argv), calls Claude Haiku CLI subprocess to extract structured infra facts (nodes+edges) as JSON, applies AnalysisPatch to owner's GraphStore in workspace/. Uses execFileSync to native CLI (not JS SDK wrapper) to avoid TypeError issues.

nodes.label: bot-scheduler (Go binary)
nodes.description: Go daemon running inside guest Docker containers. Reads /workspace/.schedule.yaml, evaluates 5-field cron expressions each minute, executes commands with configurable timeout, sends result notification to host via NOTIFY_BRIDGE_URL (http://172.18.0.1:3849/notify). Also watches /workspace/.tasks/ for background task completion (.status files).

nodes.label: scheduler cmd.go helper
nodes.description: Helper for scheduler: newCmd() creates exec.Cmd with cwd=/workspace, combined stdout+stderr piped to io.Writer.

nodes.label: .schedule.yaml
nodes.description: Per-guest cron schedule config at /workspace/.schedule.yaml. Fields: name, cron (5-field), cmd ([]string), notify (bool), timeout (seconds). Read by bot-scheduler each tick.

nodes.label: Notify Bridge (http://172.18.0.1:3849/notify)
nodes.description: HTTP endpoint on host (port 3849) that receives {userId, message} JSON from guest containers and delivers notifications via Telegram bot.

nodes.label: .daemons.yaml
nodes.description: Per-guest daemon config at /opt/vault/{userId}/.daemons.yaml. Lists always-on daemons (e.g. bot-scheduler) to run in container. Managed by migrate-scheduler.ts and container daemon runner.

nodes.label: GraphStore
nodes.description: Owner's memory knowledge graph store. Used by import-handoff.ts to load, apply patches, and save. Methods: load(), applyAnalysisPatch(), save().

nodes.label: renderHowToSetupGuide()
nodes.description: Template function generating guest public/index.html setup guide. Used by migrate-guest-public-index.ts.

nodes.label: generateGuestClaudeMd()
nodes.description: Template function generating per-guest CLAUDE.md content. Takes userId and vaultDir as arguments. Used by migrate-guest-claude-md.ts.

nodes.label: /opt/vault
nodes.description: Root directory for all guest vaults. Each subdirectory is a numeric userId. Contains CLAUDE.md, public/index.html, .daemons.yaml, skills/, .schedule.yaml, .tasks/.

nodes.label: /workspace/.tasks/
nodes.description: Per-guest background task drop-box inside container. Files: {id}.run (command), {id}.status (done|error|running), {id}.log (output). Watched by bot-scheduler's watchTasks goroutine.

properties.description: Index of the @proboiAI_bot guide library: 10 sections covering bot capabilities, MCP tools, Composio, custom MCPs, skills, FAQ
Writing style rules for guide contributors: simple Russian, no AI-isms, concrete examples, structure rules
key_rules: Facts verified against CLAUDE.md and knowledge graph

properties.description: User-facing documentation of currently active MCP tools in the bot
Roadmap of MCP integrations planned for future: Composio, Hetzner, Supabase, Playwright, Notion

nodes.label: Composio Google Workspace MCP — User Guide

properties.description: Full user guide for Composio integration: OAuth flow, supported apps, ready-made scenarios, security, limits

nodes.norm_label: composio google workspace mcp — user guide

nodes.label: Custom MCP — Writing Your Own Tool

properties.description: Guide for users wanting to create their own MCP: why, minimal TypeScript skeleton, how to add to bot, checklist

nodes.norm_label: custom mcp — writing your own tool

properties.description: Answers to frequent user questions: limits, tiers, voice messages, large files, session resume, interrupts, security
Before executing destructive tasks, Claude outputs a PLAN_START..PLAN_END block; bot pauses and shows inline buttons: Execute / Cancel / Clarify
properties.plan_mode_enabled_for: owner by default; guests with planMode flag in profile

properties.description: Claude emits TODO_LIST_START/TODO_ITEM/TODO_START/TODO_DONE markers; bot maintains a live Telegram service message showing task progress

nodes.label: Feature: Context Compaction — Auto-compress Near Limit

properties.description: When session history approaches token limit (threshold 0.8), bot summarizes old messages into a compact block and continues transparently

nodes.norm_label: feature: context compaction — auto-compress near limit

nodes.label: Feature: Guest Memory — Persistent Context Between Sessions

properties.description: After each session Claude saves a summary to /opt/vault/{userId}/memory/; injected at next session start. Guest-only (owner already has ~/.claude memory).

nodes.norm_label: feature: guest memory — persistent context between sessions

nodes.label: Feature: Interrupt with Redirect — Graceful Execution Control

properties.description: !<text> interrupts current execution, saves partial context, and immediately resumes with the redirect instruction
properties.dependency: reliable abort behavior in current SDK version

properties.description: Claude asks clarifying questions with inline button choices; bot detects tool prefix and renders Telegram keyboard

risks: LLM marker compliance: DeepSeek and Claude behave differently with special markers
Stream parsing: markers may split across chunks; need rolling buffer
Telegram edit rate limit: ~0.5 edits/second per message; need debounce
File race conditions: session files need mutex per userId
Memory size: 50KB per guest, need rotation

properties.description: User tells bot to connect app → bot sends OAuth URL → user authorizes → bot confirms; per-user isolation enforced by Composio
40-line TypeScript MCP skeleton using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk with tools/list and tools/call handlers via StdioServerTransport

steps: Place MCP folder in repo next to existing MCPs
Add tool permissions to /root/.claude/settings.json under permissions.allow

commands.!<text>: interrupt and redirect with new instruction

nodes.label: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
nodes.description: Standard protocol for attaching external tool-programs ('hands') to Claude. Each MCP is a live process that Claude calls to act in the real world (API requests, image generation, browser control, etc.)

nodes.label: Skill (Claude Code skill)
nodes.description: Markdown instruction file in ~/.claude/skills/ that modifies bot behavior for certain task types. Not a program — just text the bot reads before a task. Auto-triggered by context.

nodes.label: Guest Container (isolated env)
nodes.description: Each user gets a personal isolated Docker container on the server. Stores user files, projects, DBs. No cross-user access. 24/7 uptime.

nodes.label: BotFather Token
nodes.description: Telegram bot authentication token obtained from @BotFather. Format: digits:AlphaNum35. Must be kept secret; revocable via /revoke.

nodes.label: New User Onboarding Flow
nodes.description: 6-step new user intro: bot asks name, occupation, intended use. Responses stored and used as context. Can be skipped by sending a task directly.

nodes.label: Access Approval Flow
nodes.description: Bot is closed-access. User sends /start → owner notified → owner approves → user gets access message. Container auto-created on approval.

nodes.label: ! Interrupt Shorthand
nodes.description: Sending a message starting with ! interrupts the current bot operation and treats the rest as a new instruction. Faster than /stop + retry.

nodes.label: Scenario: One-prompt landing page
nodes.description: Bot writes HTML/CSS/JS for a one-page site, places files in user container. No additional MCPs needed.

nodes.label: Scenario: PDF summarization
nodes.description: Send PDF → bot extracts text via pdftotext, summarizes, translates, or tables key points. Built-in, no MCP needed.

nodes.label: Scenario: Voice note to structured text
nodes.description: Send voice → Whisper transcribes → bot restructures as note, post, or saved file. No extra MCPs.
nodes.norm_label: scenario: voice note to structured text

nodes.label: Scenario: Photo / screenshot analysis
nodes.description: Send photo → bot reads text, splits bills, explains errors, translates. Vision built-in via OpenRouter Gemini.

nodes.label: Scenario: Image generation
nodes.description: Free image generation via Pollinations (Flux model). Saved to /tmp/pollinations/, auto-sent via send-file MCP. No API key needed.

nodes.label: Scenario: Build a custom Telegram bot
nodes.description: Bot writes code, explains BotFather token setup, runs it in user container. Python and Node.js pre-installed.
nodes.norm_label: scenario: build a custom telegram bot

nodes.label: Scenario: Web scraping
nodes.description: Collect data from web pages via HTTP + HTML parsing. Works for static HTML now; Playwright MCP planned for JS-heavy sites.

nodes.label: Scenario: Notion integration
nodes.description: Planned via Composio MCP: create/edit/search Notion pages and databases. Not yet available.

nodes.label: Scenario: Gmail and Google Calendar
nodes.description: Planned via Composio MCP: read/send emails, create calendar events. No GCP account needed.

nodes.label: Scenario: Clone and run GitHub repo
nodes.description: Bot clones repo into user container, installs deps, runs it. git/npm/pip/bun pre-installed.
nodes.norm_label: scenario: clone and run github repo

nodes.label: Scenario: Automation scripts
nodes.description: Rename files in bulk, merge CSVs, extract links from HTML. Bot writes and immediately executes the script.

nodes.label: Scenario: Learning to program
nodes.description: Bot explains code line-by-line, debugs errors, gives practice problems and reviews solutions.

nodes.label: Scenario: Document drafting
nodes.description: Draft contracts, reports, social media posts, resumes. Iterative editing with bot.

nodes.label: Scenario: Data analysis
nodes.description: Send CSV/Excel → bot loads into pandas, finds anomalies, builds charts with matplotlib, explains findings.

nodes.label: Skill: avoid-ai-writing
nodes.description: Removes AI-stamp phrases from text, makes prose more natural. Triggers on post/article/letter writing tasks.

nodes.label: Skill: simplify
nodes.description: Simplifies code: removes unnecessary abstractions, reduces bloat.

nodes.label: Skill: init
nodes.description: Generates CLAUDE.md for a new project — instructions file for Claude about how to work with this codebase.

nodes.label: Skill: review
nodes.description: Reviews pull requests: finds bugs, bad style, common mistakes.

nodes.label: Skill: security-review
nodes.description: Scans code for vulnerabilities: SQL injection, secret leaks to logs, insecure password storage.

nodes.label: Own-bot use case: Family reminders
nodes.description: Birthday reminder bot: adds people with dates, sends reminder a week before with gift ideas.

nodes.label: Own-bot use case: Small business orders
nodes.description: Order-intake bot: accepts orders in chat, saves to Google Sheets, sends owner notification per order.

nodes.label: Own-bot use case: Workout tracker
nodes.description: Fitness tracker bot: logs exercises/weights, tracks progress, draws charts, sends reminders.

nodes.label: Own-bot use case: Team admin bot
nodes.description: Workgroup admin bot: answers FAQs, creates Trello tasks, collects status updates.
nodes.norm_label: own-bot use case: team admin bot

nodes.label: Own-bot use case: Personal diary bot
nodes.description: Voice-to-diary bot: voice event → categorized by tags/emotions → saved to Notion.
nodes.norm_label: own-bot use case: personal diary bot

nodes.label: Bot hosting via systemd in container
nodes.description: Custom bots run in user container on server 24/7. Can be wrapped in systemd service for auto-restart on crash.

nodes.label: Firewall Setup Guide
nodes.description: Firewall and egress monitoring for guest Docker containers. Blocks TCP/25, rate-limits TCP/465+587 (30 conn/hr), throttles containers at 20 GB/day egress via tc htb to 100 KB/s. Sends Telegram alerts.

nodes.label: SMTP Block Rule (TCP/25)
nodes.description: Blocks TCP/25 (SMTP) from 172.17.0.0/16 Docker subnet; logs via NFLOG group 25.

nodes.label: SMTP Rate Limit (TCP/465+587)
nodes.description: Rate-limits TCP/465 and TCP/587 to max 30 new connections per hour per container.

nodes.label: Daily Egress Throttle (20 GB cap)
nodes.description: Counts per-container daily egress; at 20 GB throttles to 100 KB/s via tc htb. Counters reset at midnight. State in /var/lib/claude-firewall/.

nodes.label: DOCKER-USER Chain Mirror Rules
nodes.description: docker-user-rules.sh mirrors INPUT-chain DROP rules into DOCKER-USER chain (defense-in-depth). Blocks ports 22, 3847, 3848 on claude-guest0 interface. Idempotent via iptables -C guard.

nodes.label: Firewall Install Script
nodes.description: scripts/firewall/install.sh — main installer. After install, configure /etc/claude-firewall/env with TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and OWNER_PROBLEM_CHANNEL_ID.

nodes.label: setup-firewall.sh (INPUT chain)
nodes.description: scripts/firewall/setup-firewall.sh — applies INPUT chain rules. Run before docker-user-rules.sh.

nodes.label: docker-user-rules.sh
nodes.description: scripts/firewall/docker-user-rules.sh — applies DOCKER-USER mirror rules. Idempotent. Added as ExecStartPre in claude-tg-bot.service for persistence across Docker restarts.

nodes.label: Firewall Uninstall Script
nodes.description: scripts/firewall/uninstall.sh — removes firewall rules and services. Optionally removes /var/lib/claude-firewall and /etc/claude-firewall.

nodes.label: Firewall State Files
nodes.description: Runtime state: /var/lib/claude-firewall/egress-*.byte (daily egress counters), throttled-*.flag (throttle markers), /var/log/claude-egress.log. Systemd units: claude-firewall.service, claude-egress-monitor.service.

nodes.label: Daemon-Runner Manual Smoke Test
nodes.description: Step-by-step manual smoke test for the daemon-runner PID 1 process in guest containers. Tests: process supervision, log output, crash restart, crashloop detection, manifest-driven removal, graceful shutdown.

nodes.label: Daemon-Runner as PID 1
nodes.description: daemon-runner runs as PID 1 inside guest Docker containers (claude-user-sandbox image). Reads .daemons.yaml manifest from vault dir. Supervises child processes, restarts on crash.

nodes.label: .daemons.yaml Manifest
nodes.description: YAML manifest at $VAULT_DIR/.daemons.yaml. Defines list of daemons with fields: name, cmd (array), workdir, enabled. daemon-runner polls this file and reconciles live processes to manifest.

nodes.label: Crashloop Detection
nodes.description: After 3 rapid crashes daemon transitions to STOPPED state (crashloop). Backoff sequence: 1s+2s+5s ≈ 8s total. Writes crashloop event JSON to .daemons-events/<name>-crashloop.json with timestamp, daemon name, last_excerpt.

nodes.label: Daemon Log Files
nodes.description: Per-daemon logs at $VAULT_DIR/logs/<daemon-name>.log. Log entries include [START], [EXIT], [RESTART] markers. Events directory at $VAULT_DIR/.daemons-events/.

nodes.label: Daemon-Runner Graceful Shutdown
nodes.description: On SIGTERM (docker stop): daemon-runner SIGTERMs all children, waits up to 10s, then exits. Logs: 'shutdown signal received' and 'all daemons stopped, exiting'.

nodes.label: Manifest-Driven Reconciliation
nodes.description: daemon-runner watches .daemons.yaml; removing a daemon entry causes the running process to be stopped within ~10s. Setting daemons: [] stops all children.

nodes.label: CPU Monitor Setup Guide
nodes.description: claude-cpu-monitor: monitors CPU usage of guest Docker containers. Runs every minute, keeps 60-reading (1 hour) history. Alerts owner channel + user DM if avg CPU > 70% over 60 min.

nodes.label: CPU Monitor Install Script
nodes.description: scripts/monitoring/install.sh — idempotent installer. Depends on Docker (docker stats) and /etc/claude-firewall/env for TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and OWNER_PROBLEM_CHANNEL_ID.

nodes.label: CPU Monitor State Files
nodes.description: State: /var/lib/claude-cpu-monitor/<container>.state (CSV, last 60 readings), <container>.notified (cooldown 6h timestamp), /var/log/claude-cpu-monitor.log.

nodes.label: CPU Alert Logic
nodes.description: Alert fires when 60-min average CPU > 70% (configurable via CPU_THRESHOLD). Cooldown 6 hours per container (via .notified file). Notifies owner channel AND user DM.

nodes.label: Skill: OCR Image
nodes.description: Skill: recognize text from image. Trigger: 'распознай текст', 'что написано', 'прочитай документ', 'скан'. Uses tesseract CLI with rus+eng. Optionally post-processes with Python for structured output.

nodes.label: Tesseract OCR Command
nodes.description: Command: tesseract image.jpg /tmp/ocr_result -l rus+eng. Output: /tmp/ocr_result.txt.

nodes.label: Skill: Create Telegram Bot
nodes.description: Skill: scaffold and deploy a user's personal Telegram bot. Trigger: 'сделай мне бота', 'хочу своего телеграм-бота'. Limit: max 3 user bots simultaneously. Steps: get token via BotFather, scaffold Python/Bun project, register in .daemons.yaml, verify startup.

nodes.label: Bot Creation Limit (3 max)
nodes.description: Maximum 3 user bots simultaneously. Check: grep -c 'name:' /workspace/.daemons.yaml — if ≥4 (including scheduler), prompt user to disable an old bot first.

nodes.label: Python Echo Bot Template
nodes.description: Minimal Python bot using python-telegram-bot: /start handler + text echo. BOT_TOKEN from env. Deployed via pip install python-telegram-bot.

nodes.label: Bun/TypeScript Bot Template
nodes.description: Minimal Bun/TypeScript bot using grammY: /start handler + text echo. BOT_TOKEN from env. Deployed via bun add grammy.

nodes.label: Bot Daemon Registration Step
nodes.description: Adds bot entry to /workspace/.daemons.yaml with name, cmd, workdir, env.BOT_TOKEN. daemon-runner picks it up in ~5s. Logs at /workspace/logs/<bot-name>.log.

nodes.label: Skill: Voice Note to Report
nodes.description: Skill: convert voice note (>30s) to structured report/protocol. Trigger: voice + 'отчёт', 'протокол', 'записки', 'резюме встречи'. Input is pre-transcribed text. Structures into key points/decisions/owners. Saves to vault/reports/report_YYYYMMDD.md, optionally converts to docx via python-docx, delivers via mcp__send-file.

nodes.label: Skill: Receipt Photo to Table
nodes.description: Skill: extract receipt data from photo into spreadsheet. Trigger: 'фото чека', 'распознай чек', 'обработай чеки', 'сколько потратил'. Uses tesseract (rus), parses item+price patterns with Python, saves to Excel via openpyxl or pandas. Delivers via mcp__send-file.

nodes.label: Skill: CSV/Excel Data Analysis
nodes.description: Skill: analyze uploaded CSV or Excel file. Trigger: .csv/.xlsx + 'проанализируй', 'что интересного', 'статистика'. Uses pandas (read_csv/read_excel), prints describe/dtypes/head, checks for nulls and ranges. Optionally plots via matplotlib. Delivers files via mcp__send-file.

nodes.label: /etc/claude-firewall/env Config
nodes.description: Shared env config for both firewall and cpu-monitor services. Contains TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and OWNER_PROBLEM_CHANNEL_ID.

nodes.label: Guest Docker Container (claude-user-sandbox)
nodes.description: Docker image claude-user-sandbox:latest. Runs daemon-runner as PID 1. Guest vault mounted at /opt/vault/<userId>/. VAULT_DIR env var set at container start.

nodes.label: Guest Vault Directory
nodes.description: /opt/vault/<userId>/ bind-mounted into guest containers. Contains .daemons.yaml, logs/, .daemons-events/, and workspace content.

nodes.label: Tesseract OCR Tool
nodes.description: CLI tool preinstalled in guest container sandbox. Supports rus+eng language packs. Used by both OCR skill and receipt-to-table skill.

nodes.label: Pandas Data Analysis Library
nodes.description: Python pandas preinstalled in guest container sandbox. Used for CSV/Excel analysis and receipt parsing.

nodes.label: openpyxl Excel Library
nodes.description: Python openpyxl preinstalled in guest container sandbox. Used by receipt-to-table skill to write Excel output.

nodes.label: python-docx Library
nodes.description: Python python-docx preinstalled in guest container sandbox. Used by voice-to-report skill to convert report to .docx format.

nodes.label: Background Tasks Skill
nodes.description: Skill for running long-running tasks (>60s) in the background using nohup + .status file pattern. Handles PDF analysis, video/audio download, large site parsing, model training, report generation.
nodes.pattern: nohup bash with .log and .status files under /workspace/.tasks/
constraints: background tasks stop on container restart

nodes.label: YouTube Summary Skill
nodes.description: Skill for summarizing YouTube videos. Downloads subtitles via yt-dlp, falls back to audio transcription with Whisper if no subtitles, parses .vtt/.srt, and produces a structured summary.

nodes.label: PDF to Excel Skill
nodes.description: Skill for extracting tables from PDF files and exporting to Excel (.xlsx). Uses pdftotext for text PDFs or pdfplumber+openpyxl for table extraction, then delivers via send-file MCP.

nodes.label: Text to Presentation Skill
nodes.description: Skill for generating PowerPoint presentations (.pptx) from text descriptions. Uses python-pptx to create slides and delivers via send-file MCP.

nodes.label: yt-dlp CLI tool
nodes.description: CLI tool for downloading YouTube subtitles and audio. Preinstalled in the guest sandbox image.

nodes.label: pdfplumber Python library
nodes.description: Python library for extracting tables and text from PDF files. Preinstalled in the guest sandbox image.

nodes.label: python-pptx Python library
nodes.description: Python library for creating and editing .pptx PowerPoint files. Preinstalled in the guest sandbox image.

nodes.label: openpyxl Python library
nodes.description: Python library for reading and writing Excel (.xlsx) files. Preinstalled in the guest sandbox image.

nodes.label: /workspace/.tasks/ directory
nodes.description: Directory pattern used by background tasks skill to store .log and .status files for tracking running background jobs.
